hook.text: Travis Kalanick and Garrett Camp couldn't get a cab in Paris in 2008. That frustration became a $70 billion company.

buildup.text: The taxi industry was broken: no transparency, no tracking, cash only, and no accountability for bad service.

discovery.text: Uber's product innovation was the map. Seeing your driver approach in real time eliminated the anxiety of 'where is my ride?'

twist.text: Uber didn't start with a technology breakthrough. GPS and smartphones already existed. The product was a better experience layer.

climax.text: Uber proved that product experience improvements alone can disrupt entire industries — even heavily regulated ones.

punchline.text: Same ride. Better experience. That was enough to change everything.

quiz.question: What was Uber's core product innovation?
options: A better experience layer — real-time tracking, cashless payment, and accountability
